title: Transverse tensile and fatigue properties of Fe-Mn-Al-C alloys
abstract_summary: This study investigates the transverse tensile and fatigue properties of three Fe-29Mn-9Al alloys with varying carbon contents (0.26–1.1 wt%) leading to different austenite/ferrite phase fractions. The mechanical behavior under transverse stress is compared with longitudinal results, focusing on ultimate tensile strength, yield strength, elongation, and S-N fatigue curves. The research aims to elucidate the effect of ferrite phase morphology and orientation on fatigue crack initiation and propagation mechanisms.
research_objective: To evaluate and compare the transverse tensile and fatigue behavior of Fe-Mn-Al-C alloys with different carbon contents and phase fractions, and to clarify the role of ferrite phase morphology in crack propagation anisotropy.
main_process_variables: phase fraction (fully austenite, 90% austenite/10% ferrite, 45% austenite/55% ferrite)
